parsed_review.summary: CP00-619 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (RP20 Data Room/VDR P02 Claude-review tail + P03 API/interface bridge; 150 units; risk C) and is the correct live-queued pack after CP00-618 — its closeout-pack-plan.json entry, source_ledger_sha, range RP20.P02.M09.S09-RP20.P03.M06.S12, risk class, and unit count all match the baseline (deviation_from_plan:false). Static read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms registry binding, service.js descriptor, validators.js, contract projections, model.test.js, the rp20 validator script, manifest, and README are mutually consistent. Coverage reconciles to 150 units / 9 sections with deliverable counts matching the plan, and the no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime attestation chain (freezeCp610Result→freezeCp619Result) supplies every flag the validator asserts; the validator returns production_ready_candidate only after all boundary assertions pass, and tests re-assert the same. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only and explicitly not final approver, human final approval is required, and local validation does not claim enterprise trust; Hermes is descriptor-only (no runtime receipts, not embedded in product runtime). The pack does not over-claim: manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication all honestly report in_progress/pending_review/production_ready:false/runtime_ready:false. Auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON; no invalid review attempts or forbidden review evidence are present for this pack; unrelated dirty files (LDIP invalid attempt, workbook docs, progress-control-room) are correctly excluded from scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. Two P3 informational notes: (1) production_ready closeout remains gated on the validation-command suite being recorded green plus a valid receipt and human final approval — command-evidence currently records zero commands by design pre-receipt; (2) this descriptor pack opens no product runtime, so closeout here means descriptor_verified, not runtime/enterprise readiness. Verdict: PASS — this read-only review does not itself block pack closeout; final production_ready and the enterprise-trust claim remain a human decision outside this receipt.

findings.title: Production_ready closeout remains gated after this review (command evidence not yet populated)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records "commands": [] with status "pending" and gate_outcome "pending_pack_evidence_creation"; production_ready is false across manifest.json, command-evidence.json, and construction-inspection.json. The descriptor pack itself is sound and internally consistent, but production_ready closeout still requires the listed validation suite (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) recorded as passing, exactly one valid review receipt from this run, and human final approval for the enterprise-trust claim. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) I verified by static inspection and could not execute those commands; the pack makes no claim that they passed, so no unsupported claim exists.
findings.recommendation: Before any production_ready flip, populate command-evidence.json with the executed validation commands and their pass results, persist exactly one valid review receipt, and obtain human final approval. No source change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-receipt sequencing; not a defect. Informational for adjudication.

findings.title: Descriptor-only pack opens no product runtime (runtime_ready false) — by design
findings.details: manifest.json sets implementation_layer "descriptor", runtime_opened false, and runtime_ready false; the descriptor, validators, and tests assert every dispatches_* and executes_* runtime flag is false. CP00-619 closeout therefore means data_room_vdr_core_p02_claude_p03_api_interface_bridge_descriptor_verified (descriptor coverage of the P02 Claude tail and P03 API/interface bridge) and does not make the Data Room/VDR product executable. This is consistent with the program design where runtime opens in later packs.
findings.recommendation: Continue treating production_ready here as descriptor verification only; do not interpret it as runtime or enterprise readiness. No change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected per program design; not a defect. Informational.
